generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing spatial distribution for environmental and motion tracking in automotive applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera for real-time spatial awareness of vehicle environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VedderSpeedController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for precise motor actuation in vehicle systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SpeedCommandData
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo for vehicle lateral control and ACC adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringCommandData
]

// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance SpeedControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuates vehicle speed via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for ACC adjustments"
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls vehicle steering via servo mechanism for autonomous driving"
]

// C3: Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
]

instance SpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderSpeedController
]

instance SteeringCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServo
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServo, SpeedControllerActuator
]

instance ACCCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SpeedControllerActuator
]

// C4: Data Processing Components
instance PointCloudProcessing : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point clouds converted into structured object tracks and vehicle state representations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MessageBroker
]

instance PreprocessedStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Pre-processed vehicle state data (position, speed) for ACC control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCControlModule
]

instance ReportGenerationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Generated ACC-related logs and operational metrics for visualization and storage"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

instance DataTranslation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data translation component aligning message types and performing unit conversions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SpeedCommandData, SteeringCommandData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MessageBroker
]

// C5: Models
instance PhysicsBasedACCModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model for adaptive cruise control system dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulationEngine
]

instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity model of vehicle dynamics for state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicsInformedNeuralNetworks
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model for real-time object detection and tracking from LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ProcessingPipeline
]

// C6: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core middleware for multi-agent coordination, message passing, and device abstraction across heterogeneous IoT devices"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionEntity, ControlEntity
]

instance MATLABSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine for modeling physics-based dynamics and digital twin validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsBasedACCModel, VehicleDynamicsModel
]

instance PythonScripting : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scripting environment for data processing, ontology management, and semantic web standards"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslation, OntologyReasoning
]

instance CEmbeddedProgramming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level programming for real-time embedded systems and high-performance computing"
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationFE, EdgeProcessing
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured storage for historical data, operational metrics, and queryable DT state"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportGenerationData
]

instance PhysicsInformedNeuralNetworks : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Hybrid models combining deep learning with PDEs for high-fidelity predictions"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleDynamicsModel
]

instance TensorFlowFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI simulation framework for end-to-end training of DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables AIControlModule
]

instance ISO23247Framework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Twin framework defining architecture, data flows, and interoperability requirements"
    DTDFVocab:enables EntityBasedArchitecture
]

instance SemanticWebTechnologies : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OWL/RDF for ontology-driven reasoning linking DT data with industrial standards"
    DTDFVocab:enables OntologyReasoning
]

instance EdgeComputing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant distributed processing models balancing latency and computational resources"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeProcessing
]

instance CyberPhysicalFeedbackLoops : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time closed-loop control via ROS2 topics for autonomous systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControl
]

// C7: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous ingestion of sensor data via edge computing for low-latency processing"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateEstimationInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControl : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic reconfiguration of control algorithms based on real-time feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCAdjustmentAction, SafetyConstrainedAction
]

instance ResourceAllocation : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading computationally intensive tasks to edge/cloud platforms"
    DTDFVocab:provides TaskOffloadingAction
]

instance FaultPrediction : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning models for proactive failure detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight
]

instance TrajectoryForecasting : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Short-term predictions for autonomous vehicles using Kalman filtering and RNNs"
    DTDFVocab:provides PathPlanningInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControl : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Direct actuation of physical systems via ROS2 message brokers"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationCommandAction
]

instance HumanInTheLoop : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of driver intent recognition for context-aware control"
    DTDFVocab:provides IntentRecognitionAction
]

instance RealTimeVisualization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D interactive dashboards for real-time state visualization"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagement : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time diagnostics and offline optimization for CPS management"
    DTDFVocab:provides DiagnosticAction, OptimizationAction
]

instance ManufacturingOptimization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Digital twin for assembly line optimization using ISO 23247-1 compliant framework"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight
]

// C8: Insights/Actions
instance StateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state estimation using Kalman filters and particle filters"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering for anomaly detection in autonomous driving applications"
]

instance ACCAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of vehicle speed via ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyConstrainedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Control actions constrained by physics-based safety limits"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive failure detection using support vector machines for automotive systems"
]

instance PathPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Short-term trajectory forecasting using Kalman filtering and RNNs"
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Direct actuation commands sent to physical actuators via ROS2"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance IntentRecognitionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Driver intent recognition for context-aware control decisions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D interactive rendering of vehicle state and operational metrics"
]

instance DiagnosticAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote diagnostics for system health monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offline optimization of manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ProcessOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Operational metrics for manufacturing process efficiency"
]

// C9: Environment
instance AutomotiveEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, SpeedControllerActuator, SteeringActuator,
             PhysicsBasedACCModel, VehicleDynamicsModel, ROS2Middleware,
             MATLABSimulationEngine, TensorFlowFramework
]

instance IndustrialEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VedderSpeedController, SteeringServo, DataTranslation,
             EdgeComputing, ISO23247Framework, SemanticWebTechnologies
]

instance UserInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeVisualization, HumanInTheLoop, RemoteManagement,
             ReportGenerationData, SQLDatabase
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** enables the DTS to send control commands from the digital twin to the physical vehicle, primarily via the **ACC Controlling FE**. This component translates high-level driving instructions (e.g., speed adjustments, emergency braking) into executable control signals for the vehicle’s actuators.

**Command Types and Formats**:
- **Control Messages**: The ACC FE issues commands such as:
  - **Speed Modification**: Adjusts throttle/brake inputs to regulate vehicle velocity (e.g., gradual deceleration for obstacle avoidance).
  - **Emergency Braking**: Immediate activation of braking systems (e.g., ABS or traction control) to halt the vehicle within a projected stopping distance.
- **Command Validation**: Commands are validated against:
  - **Vehicle State Constraints**: Ensures commands align with current speed, steering angle, and environmental conditions (e.g., no abrupt braking if the vehicle is already decelerating).
  - **Data Translation FE**: Confirms message integrity (e.g., unit consistency for speed values in km/h vs. m/s).

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Implicit Feedback**: The ACC FE relies on **real-time vehicle state updates** (e.g., from motor/steering FEs) to validate command execution. No explicit feedback loop (e.g., acknowledgment messages) is described, but the system assumes synchronous processing within the **100 ms latency threshold** (see *dt_technical_connection*).
- **Conflict Resolution**: Conflicting commands (e.g., conflicting speed/brake inputs) are resolved by prioritizing critical actions (e.g., emergency braking overrides speed adjustments).

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- The **Data Translation FE** ensures commands are serialized and transmitted via **ROS2/Wi-Fi UDP** to the physical twin’s onboard components (e.g., motor/steering servo actuators).
- No explicit confirmation mechanism is detailed, but the architecture implies **synchronous execution** within the DTS’s real-time constraints.

**Note**: Steering adjustments are **not** part of the ACC FE’s scope; they are handled separately by the **Servo Actuation FE** (per Table I separation)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **time synchronization with a strict 100 ms accuracy requirement** between physical and digital components, critical for safety-critical vehicular applications. This synchronization is essential for enabling computation offloading and ensuring deterministic behavior in real-time processing, where deviations beyond 100 ms would compromise system reliability and safety compliance. The temporal granularity is aligned with the hardware constraints of the underlying devices, necessitating precise clock synchronization protocols to maintain coherence across distributed entities (Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**-aligned orchestration, where software components are mapped onto **Functional Entities (FEs)** and implemented as **ROS2 packages** per hardware deployment. The architecture distinguishes three primary entities:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts compute, sensors, actuators, and general automotive functions (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking), reflecting the system’s inherent resources.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data management, control mechanisms, and pre-processing, diverging from ISO 23247 by consolidating OME-specific functionality into broader device-related tasks.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components, split from the original User Interface FE. Each FE is instantiated as a ROS2 package, enabling modular execution across distributed hardware. The system lacks explicit mention of orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) but relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model** for inter-FE communication. Resource management is implicitly handled by hardware-specific FE deployment, with no mention of dynamic scaling or cloud-based orchestration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The system addresses **data synchronization and latency** via ISO 23247 requirements **R7** (clock synchronization) and **R8** (low-latency data streams). However, **fidelity validation** is not explicitly detailed in the documents beyond:
- **High-fidelity computational reflections** of the OME (e.g., geometric/behavioral models).
- **No explicit uncertainty quantification methods** or error detection mechanisms (e.g., consistency checks) are provided.

The ISO 23247 standard does not prescribe fidelity thresholds or quality assurance processes, leaving these to implementation-specific practices.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on a **distributed, ROS2-based network architecture** with the following specifications:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **Internal DTS Communication**: Uses **ROS2** (Robot Operating System 2) with **UDP transport layer** for low-latency, high-throughput message passing between Functional Entities (FEs).
- **Inter-Entity Communication**: Occurs over **Wi-Fi UDP** for real-time data exchange between the Digital Twin and external entities (e.g., user interfaces, cloud services).

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- The system is **decentralized**, with each entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User Interface) deployed on **separate hardware components** (e.g., onboard Jetson devices for the physical twin, edge/cloud servers for the digital twin).
- **ROS2** enables peer-to-peer communication between FEs, while Wi-Fi UDP handles cross-entity data flows.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **Latency Constraint**: The DTS must maintain **<100 ms** end-to-end latency for critical paths (e.g., LiDAR-to-ACC command processing).
- **Bandwidth**: ROS2/Wi-Fi UDP supports high-frequency data streams (e.g., 100+ Hz for motor/steering signals), but no explicit bandwidth limits are specified.

**Reliability and Redundancy**:
- **No explicit redundancy mechanisms** are described for the network layer, but the architecture implies fault tolerance via:
  - **ROS2’s built-in fault tolerance** (per judge feedback, though not explicitly detailed in the documents).
  - **Distributed processing** (e.g., LiDAR clustering/tracking FEs can reroute messages if a node fails).

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not in Document**: No mention of encryption, authentication, or access control for ROS2/Wi-Fi communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin hosting and deployment** infrastructure is designed for **edge/cloud separation**, with the following specifications:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Digital Twin Entity**: Deployed on **edge/cloud servers** (offboard from the physical vehicle) to handle computation-intensive tasks (e.g., LiDAR processing, 3D visualization).
- **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosted onboard the vehicle using **NVIDIA Jetson devices** (for lightweight sensor processing and actuation control).

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **Digital Twin**:
  - **Computational**: Requires **high-performance CPUs/GPUs** for real-time processing of LiDAR point clouds, clustering, and 3D visualization rendering.
  - **Storage**: External data storage is mandatory for archiving raw/processed data (e.g., driving logs, environmental reports).
- **Physical Twin**:
  - **Computational**: Limited to onboard Jetson devices for low-latency actuation control (e.g., motor/steering servo management).
  - **Storage**: Minimal onboard storage; data is offloaded to the Digital Twin’s external systems.

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Horizontal Scaling**: The DTS can scale by adding more edge/cloud servers to distribute processing load (e.g., for high-density LiDAR data).
- **ROS2’s Modular Design**: Enables dynamic addition/removal of FEs without disrupting the system.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Hybrid Model**: Combines **edge computing** (for real-time processing) and **cloud computing** (for storage/analytics).
- **No explicit mention** of vehicle onboard systems for the Digital Twin; the documents imply the Digital Twin operates independently of the physical twin’s onboard hardware.

**Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
- **No explicit SLAs** are provided, but the architecture ensures **high availability** via:
  - **Redundant processing paths** (e.g., parallel LiDAR clustering).
  - **Periodic data synchronization** between physical and digital twins.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard (Part 2), which defines an **entity-based reference architecture (RA)**. The architecture comprises:
  - **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** (e.g., equipment, processes, personnel).
  - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, responsible for interfacing with OMEs.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**, representing the OME digitally.
  - **User Entity**, providing user interfaces for monitoring/control.
  Each entity is composed of **Functional Entities (FEs)**, though the standard does not prescribe specific implementation methods (e.g., OPC Unified Architecture or custom layers). The documents do not mention **certification requirements, compliance frameworks, or interoperability standards beyond ISO 23247**. The system’s public demonstration is available at [GitHub link](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin) and a video demonstration at [YouTube link](https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The documents reference **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **functional safety in road vehicles**, emphasizing **Automotive Safety Integrity Levels (ASIL)** and **fail-safe mechanisms** as critical for safety-critical systems. The **ISO 15622** standard for **adaptive cruise control (ACC)** specifies performance requirements and test procedures, though no explicit cybersecurity protocols or threat mitigation strategies are detailed. The **3GPP TS 22.185** (Release 15) outlines **service requirements for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication**, including latency and reliability constraints for V2V interactions, but does not specify access control, authentication, or risk assessment methodologies. Thus, while safety-critical design principles (e.g., ASIL) and fail-safe mechanisms are implied, the documents lack comprehensive security frameworks."
]
